title: Rich American Custard
description: A classic American dessert featuring vanilla custard and a chewy texture, perfect for satisfying your late-night cravings. This recipe is low-fat-friendly and easy to make.
instructions: Combine the egg yolks, sugar, and a pinch of salt in a medium bowl and whisk until pale and thick.
In a separate saucepan, whisk together the milk and vanilla extract over medium heat until warm and just simmering.
Gradually temper the egg yolk mixture by slowly pouring the warm milk mixture into the bowl, whisking constantly.
Return the mixture to the saucepan and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the custard thickens and coats the back of a spoon, about 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in the soaked gelatin until dissolved.
Strain the custard into a clean bowl and let cool to room temperature.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the custard for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a steamer basket over simmering water until hot.
Dust a large plate with a thin layer of flour and lay a sheet of parchment paper on top of it.
Uncover the chilled custard and pour it onto the prepared plate.
Place the plate in the steamer basket and steam for 20 minutes, or until the custard is set and has a firm, chewy texture.
Serve the custard warm or at room temperature, garnished with fresh fruit or whipped cream if desired.
